title: taenitis obtusa
intro: Taenitis obtusa, also known as 'Ma'o hau hele', is a species of fern native to Hawaii, known for its bright yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Taenitis obtusa include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Taenitis obtusa can be found in various forms, including tea, tinctures, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
